results.title: Top 6 Insight Alternatives To Solve Modern IT Challenges
results.content:
# TL;DR

 Insight’s offerings might be too limiting for specific industries with pricing that may be less competitive.
 Delays in service and support response times are common complaints with Insight, prompting businesses to seek alternative providers.
 CDW provides a comprehensive range of IT solutions with solid vendor partnerships.
 Zones IT excels in cloud and data center services with end-to-end IT solutions for businesses to modernize and optimize their operations.
 CoreTrust leverages group purchasing power to negotiate competitive contracts.
 Ingram Micro is a global leader in IT distribution and supply chain management.
 SHI has expertise in software licensing and next-gen infrastructure solutions, which makes it ideal for large enterprises. [...] However, as companies demand better, more value-for-money services, users feel Insight may not be the best fit for their growing needs. And one of the primary pain points customers often cite is the price being too high.

A customer unhappy with Insight’s pricing model; Via G2

This, combined with occasional service or support response times delays, has led many to explore alternative providers offering more niche solutions or better customer experiences.

A reviewer sharing their negative experience with Insight; Via G2

If you’ve faced similar challenges with Insight, it is time to consider other options.

In this article, we’ll explore some of the top alternatives to Insight. Let’s find out more.

# TL;DR [...] | SHI | Corporate, enterprise, public sector, and academic organizations. | IT products and services, cybersecurity, data optimization, and IT infrastructure solutions. | A comprehensive range of IT solutions, commitment to customer satisfaction, and largest MWBE in the US. |
| Ingram Micro | Small to midsize businesses (SMBs). | IT products distribution and supply chain management. | Integrates technology distribution with comprehensive IT services, offering end-to-end solutions to partners. |

results.title: Top 10 Insight7 Alternatives and Competitors | Balto
results.content:
Upcoming webinar: Agent Assist, Reimagined (with Balto CEO Marc Bernstein)

Save Your Seat

# Top 10 Insight7 Alternatives& Competitors 2025

Looking for alternatives or competitors to Insight7? When evaluating AI-powered research and insights platforms, consider accuracy, collaboration tools, and how well the platform turns qualitative data into actionable findings. The best Insight7 alternative is Dovetail, but other providers like Aurelius, Grain, and Balto deliver strong value depending on your goals.

See Why Teams Switch to BaltoExplore Alternatives

## What is Insight7

Insight7 is an AI-powered research and insights platform that helps teams analyze customer feedback, support calls, and other qualitative data at scale. [...] The top Insight7 competitors include Dovetail, Condens, EnjoyHQ (UserTesting), Reveall (NEXT), Grain, Fireflies, Otter, Balto, and Observe AI.

Each offers different strengths across research repositories, qualitative analysis, multi-source insight management, and real-time guidance for customer-facing teams.

Insight7 specializes in post-call qualitative synthesis (automated summaries, tagging, and theme detection) while Balto focuses on real-time agent guidance.

Balto helps agents follow best practices, reduce repeat calls, and improve  CSAT  during live conversations, turning insights into action in the moment rather than after the fact. [...] No. Insight7 is a qualitative research and insight synthesis platform, not a contact center performance solution. Leaders in AI-driven contact center software typically include Balto, Observe AI, and other platforms focused on real-time guidance, QA automation, and operational analytics.

Balto is the leader in real-time agent coaching, providing dynamic prompts, checklists, and compliance cues during live calls.

Unlike research-focused platforms like Insight7, Balto improves outcomes while the customer interaction is happening, not just in post-call analysis.

results.title: InSight - Wikipedia
results.content: The Mars Cube One (MarCO) spacecraft are a pair of 6U CubeSats that piggybacked with the InSight mission to test CubeSat navigation and endurance in deep space, and to help relay real-time communications (with an eight-minute lightspeed delay) during the probe's entry, descent and landing (EDL) phase. The two 6U CubeSats, named MarCO A and B, are identical. They were launched along with InSight, but separated soon after reaching space, and they flew as a pair for redundancy while flanking the lander. They did not enter orbit, but flew past Mars during the EDL phase of the mission and relayed InSight's telemetry in real time. The success of the MarCO spacecraft proved the viability of the cubesat platform for deep space missions and helped serve as a technical demonstration for potential [...] The InSight lander, powered by solar panels and batteries, relies on periodic wind gusts called "cleaning events" to reduce dust accumulation on the panels. Elysium Planitia, the landing site of InSight, experienced fewer cleaning events than needed to keep science operations powered. In February 2021, at the start of the Martian winter, InSight's solar cells were producing 27% of capacity due to a thick covering of dust on the panels. At that time, NASA began the process of putting the lander into hibernation mode, shutting down data-gathering instruments on a schedule to conserve enough power to keep the lander electronics warm through the Martian winter. NASA had hoped that weather conditions would improve and allow InSight to store enough energy to come out of hibernation in July [...] (spacecraft)")  Voyager Mars "Voyager program (Mars)") | | |

results.title: InSight | Lockheed Martin
results.content:
InSight Mars Lander

InSight, which stands for Interior Exploration using Seismic Investigations, Geodesy and Heat Transport, is NASA’s next Mars lander and is designed to explore the Martian interior. The InSight mission is led by NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), which has been a pioneer in Mars exploration for decades. JPL’s collaboration with Lockheed Martin goes back to the Viking lander in 1976, which was NASA’s first mission to the surface of Mars. JPL partners with both academia and private industry firms to bring the best of national and international expertise and innovation forward to make exploration missions like InSight possible.

Equipped with three primary instruments, the lander will take the first-ever in-depth look at the planet’s interior. The data will help: [...] InSight

## The Spacecraft

Lockheed Martin is the InSight prime contractor and is responsible for the complete spacecraft system – cruise stage, aeroshell and the lander itself. Based on a proven spacecraft design from the successful 2007 Phoenix mission, InSight will incorporate the latest avionics technology as well as advanced science instruments.
